posts.text:
BREAKING: US equity funds posted a record +$119 billion in inflows in the week ending June 17th.

This was driven by tech, which saw +$19.2 billion in inflows, the largest weekly intake on record.

Year-to-date, US equity funds have attracted +$330 billion in inflows, the most of any region.

By comparison, international equities have seen +$290 billion in inflows while China has recorded -$220 billion in outflows.

As a result, US equity funds are on track to pull in a record +$739 billion in 2026.

At the same time, tech funds are on pace to attract a record +$154 billion, more than double the prior annual record set in 2025.

Investors are piling into US equities at a pace never seen before.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
